MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Hunter Sims, of CCSF City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4259, for cross-defendant CCSF;
David Hunter, of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 510.457.3466, for defendant Nazzal & Nazzal Jamileh Trust.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. The City failed to maintain its initial summary judgment burden. The City presented no evidence that the sidewalk area was not in a dangerous condition at the time plaintiff Elmar Bouwman fell or that the asserted dangerous condition was not created by a negligent or wrongful act or omission of an employee of the City. Nor did the City present any evidence that either Mr. Bouwman or cross-complainant Nazzal & Nazzal Jamileh Trust do not now have and cannot reasonably obtain any evidence that the sidewalk area was in a dangerous condition at the time Mr. Bouwman fell or that the asserted dangerous condition was created by a negligent or wrongful act or omission of an employee of the City. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
